commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=43b28627f4987c49b3ea8bcccf75d36922f5bfb2)
commands.review_summary:
Changes the release workflow to dispatch Homebrew tap updates with a multi-arch artifact template and adds a `build-darwin` Make target for darwin/amd64 and darwin/arm64 builds.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main dispatches only the darwin_arm64 Homebrew artifact and the current tap formula is arm64-pinned. I did not run a release workflow.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers should coordinate the cross-repository Homebrew tap fix before merge; the sonoscli diff alone does not remove the arm64-only formula constraint.

target.title: Add support for intel macs
target.body:
The code builds (and works) for intel macs. However, there are no releases that actually get created (despite [the docs saying so](https://sonoscli.sh/install.html#prebuilt-release-archives)).

## Summary

Add Intel macOS support to the release/install path.

The GoReleaser config already included `darwin_amd64`, so the CLI itself can build for Intel macOS. The missing piece was downstream packaging: the release workflow was dispatching the Homebrew tap update with a hard-coded `darwin_arm64` artifact.

This updates the release workflow to pass the multi-arch artifact template so the tap updater can select both darwin_amd64 and darwin_arm64 artifacts.

Also adds a local make build-darwin target to build both macOS binaries explicitly.

## Verification

Verified local cross-compilation for both macOS targets:

GOOS=darwin GOARCH=amd64 CGO_ENABLED=0 go build ./cmd/sonos
GOOS=darwin GOARCH=arm64 CGO_ENABLED=0 go build ./cmd/sonos

Confirmed generated binaries report the expected Mach-O architectures: x86_64 and arm64.

## After this

Once intel builds are in the release pipeline, the next step will be to update the homebrew [formula](https://github.com/steipete/homebrew-tap) after this change lands.
target.author: streeter

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
